arxiv_papers.title: Mapping how LLMs debate societal issues when shadowing human personality traits, sociodemographics and social media behavior
arxiv_papers.snippet: Large Language Models (LLMs) can strongly shape social discourse, yet datasets investigating how LLM outputs vary across controlled social and contextual prompting remain sparse. Cognitive Digital Shadows (CDS) is a 190,000-record synthetic corpus supporting analyses of LLM-generated discourse. Each

arxiv_papers.title: Leading Across the Spectrum of Human-AI Relationships: A Conceptual Framework for Increasingly Heterogeneous Teams
arxiv_papers.snippet: What shapes a consequential decision when human and artificial intelligence work on it together? The answer is becoming harder to see. A decision may look human-led after AI has set the frame, or appear automated while human judgment still carries decisive force. This paper offers a leadership-facin

podcast: [#239 – Rose Hadshar on why automating all human labour will break our political system] The most important political question in the age of advanced AI might not be who wins elections.
